2604.25352.title: GraphPL: Leveraging GNN for Efficient and Robust Modalities Imputation in Patchwork Learning
2604.25352.paper_summary: Current research on distributed multi-modal learning typically assumes that clients can access complete information across all modalities, which may not hold in practice. In this paper, we explore patchwork learning, in which the modalities available to different clients vary, and the objective is to impute the missing modalities for each client in an unsupervised manner. Existing methods are shown not to fully utilize the modality information as they tend to rely on only a subset of the observed modalities. To address this issue, we propose GraphPL, which combines graph neural networks with patchwork learning to flexibly integrate all observed modalities and remains robust with noisy inputs. Experimental results show that GraphPL achieves SOTA performance on benchmark datasets. Our results on real-world distributed electronic health record dataset show GraphPL learns strong downstream features and enables tasks like disease prediction via superior modality imputation.
2604.25352.paper_summary_zh: 目前對於分散式多模態學習的研究通常假設客戶端可以訪問所有模態的完整信息，但在實踐中這可能並不成立。<lb/>在本文中，我們探索了拼接學習，其中不同客戶端可用的模態各不相同，目標是以無監督的方式推測每個客戶端缺失的模態。<lb/>現有方法顯示未能充分利用模態信息，因為它們往往僅依賴於觀察到的模態的子集。<lb/>為了解決這個問題，我們提出了GraphPL，它將圖神經網絡與拼接學習相結合，以靈活整合所有觀察到的模態，並在噪聲輸入下保持穩健。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，GraphPL在基準數據集上達到了SOTA性能。<lb/>我們在真實世界的分散式電子健康記錄數據集上的結果顯示，GraphPL學習了強大的下游特徵，並通過優越的模態推測實現了疾病預測等任務。
2604.25352.author: Xingjian Hu et.al.
2604.25352.authors: Xingjian Hu, Zuoyu Yan, Jianhua Zhu, Liangcai Gao, Fei Wang, Tengfei Ma
